created_by: Wilderculture WILD Toolbox session (Claude) — woodland group batch; field sheet 10 split per type. v3.3.

ukhab_level5_states.w1d5: Alder woodland on floodplains (Annex I H91E0)

ukhab_identity.ukhab_level5_note: Keyed at w1d. w1d5 (alluvial alder/willow/ash, H91E0) and w1d6 (bog woodland, H91D0) are the Annex I Level-5 types. NB bog woodland (w1d6) is a special case — on shallow peat, NOT a licence to afforest deep blanket bog (f1a).
ukhab_identity.annex1_habitat: H91E0 Alluvial alder/willow/ash forests (w1d5); H91D0 Bog woodland (w1d6)

sources.title: UK Habitat Classification v2.01 (official definitions + master data sheet + applied surveys)
sources.specific_claim: w1d = Wet woodland (w1d5 alder on floodplains H91E0, w1d6 bog woodland H91D0). Confirmed in official UKHab definitions and applied surveys.

nvc_communities: W7 Alnus glutinosa–Fraxinus excelsior–Lysimachia nemorum woodland
(W1–W3 willow carr at the wettest end)
cross_references.nvc_communities_notes: Willow, alder and birch carr on wet valley floors and flushes — the wooded end of the rush-pasture and wet-heath succession.

sources.title: NVC Field Guide to Woodland (W4, W7)
sources.specific_claim: W4 birch-Molinia and W7 alder-ash are the wet woodland communities.

ecology.description: Willow, alder and birch carr on wet valley floors and flushes — the wooded end of the rush-pasture (f2b) and wet-heath (h1b6) succession. A high-productivity tangle of swampy vegetation and dense underwood. As with all the woodland, managed by browsing level for regeneration, not stocked.
condition_indicators_good: Full age range with regeneration; standing and fallen deadwood
Intact wet-woodland ground flora and bryophytes; damp surface maintained
Browsing light enough that regeneration survives
condition_indicators_poor: Overgrazed: no regeneration; poached wet ground
Non-native conifer or invasive ingress; deadwood tidied away
ecosystem_functions: High productivity and dense structure — rich invertebrate and bird habitat
Water storage and flood attenuation on valley floors and flushes
Soil-builder and carbon store (incl. peat in bog woodland)
The wooded successional endpoint for wet open habitats
ecology.whole_system_role: The wet-end woodland climax — where rush pasture and wet heath go when released on suitable (non-deep-peat) ground.

successional_neighbours.habitat_code: f2b rush pasture / h1b6 wet heath
successional_neighbours.note: Rush pasture → wetland scrub (willow) → wet woodland; wet heath on suitable non-peat margins → bog myrtle/willow → wet woodland.
Bog woodland (w1d6) is on shallow peat; do NOT extend it onto deep blanket bog — afforesting deep peat dries and oxidises the carbon store.

sources.title: Wilderculture field sheet 10 — Native Woodland
sources.specific_claim: Wet woodland as the carr endpoint of wet-habitat succession; the deep-peat exclusion.

ukhab_condition.condition_grade_note: Assigned per parcel; regeneration and wet character are the diagnostics.

condition_grade_descriptors.label: Favourable
condition_grade_descriptors.description: Multi-aged wet woodland with regeneration; damp surface; native dominant; deadwood retained.

condition_grade_descriptors.label: Unfavourable recovering
condition_grade_descriptors.description: Browsing reduced and/or hydrology restored; regeneration returning.

condition_grade_descriptors.label: Unfavourable declining
condition_grade_descriptors.description: Overgrazed/poached or drying out; invasive/conifer ingress.

sources.title: Woodland Condition Assessment
sources.specific_claim: Regeneration and age structure are core indicators.

abiotic_template.parent_material_geology: Wet valley floors, flushes and floodplains; alluvium and shallow peat
abiotic_template.base_status: variable (neutral to acid; alder fixes N)
abiotic_template.altitude_range_m: Valley floor and flushes, below the treeline
abiotic_template.bioclimate_notes: Throughout the uplands wherever ground stays wet.

elemental_conditions.convention_note: CURRENT-STATE reading. Bioregionally Wilder / Protect & Restore (the wet wooded endpoint).

water.descriptor: The wet exception among the woodlands; damp to waterlogged.

earth.pedogenic_process: alluvial / gleying / shallow-peat with litter cycling
earth.descriptor: Soil-building under the canopy; carbon in soil, timber and (bog woodland) shallow peat. Alder fixes nitrogen.

fire.descriptor: High productivity and dense underwood; energy flows to a rich invertebrate/bird community.

range_of_natural_variability: New willow/alder colonising released wet ground
successional_envelope.successional_pathway: rush pasture / wet heath → wetland (willow) scrub → wet woodland. On deep peat the answer is REWETTING to bog, not woodland — bog woodland (w1d6) is shallow-peat only.

wild_assessment.energy_flow_role: High-productivity wet woodland; the wooded resolution of the wet-habitat succession and a flood/water-storage asset.
wild_assessment.wild_zone_rationale: The wet wooded endpoint — Bioregionally Wilder where new carr develops on released wet ground; ancient wet woodland is Protect & Restore. CRITICAL exclusion: not on deep blanket bog (f1a), where rewetting, not woodland, is the wilder move.
wild_assessment.disturbance_regime: Browsing-controlled and hydrology-led. Reduce deer/sheep for regeneration; maintain the wet character (do not drain).

wild_management_target.detail: Sustain natural regeneration by reducing browsing while keeping the ground wet (do not drain). Allow willow/alder carr to develop on released rush pasture/wet heath margins. Keep deadwood. Do NOT extend onto deep peat.

wild_assessment.ecological_value_notes: High — an Annex I priority type (H91E0/H91D0), high-productivity, structurally rich, and a flood/water-storage and carbon asset; variable by stand.

rarity_irreplaceability.notes: Priority/Annex I; alluvial and bog woodland are restricted and ancient stands irreplaceable.

diversity.notes: Dense structure and rich invertebrate/bird habitat.

energy_flow_contribution.notes: High woodland productivity plus water-regulation function.

naturalistic_lu_ha.value: Near nil — reduce browsing; keep stock off the soft wet surface
naturalistic_lu_ha.basis_note: Reduce deer/sheep for regeneration; wet ground poaches easily, so grazing pressure is doubly damaging.

wild_assessment.mosaic_role_note: The wet wooded endpoint of the valley-floor mosaic; not a grazing patch.
wild_assessment.preferential_grazing_magnet_note: Willow browse is high-value (see scrub) — regeneration fails under heavy grazing.
process_indicators: willow/alder establishment on released wet ground
wild_assessment.process_indicators_note: Hydrology and browsing together govern the outcome.

sources.title: Wilderculture field sheet 10 — Native Woodland
sources.specific_claim: Wet woodland ecology, zone, deep-peat exclusion.

forage.overall_palatability_notes: Willow/alder browse is high-value (see the scrub sheet) — the reason wet woodland fails to regenerate under heavy grazing. Not a managed forage resource, and the wet surface poaches.

forage_yield_by_grade.notes: Not a forage/stocking habitat — browsing-managed for regeneration.

conservation_lu_ha.value: Near nil — reduce browsing; keep stock off the soft wet surface
conservation_lu_ha.scheme: site-specific; not a standard stocking figure
conservation_lu_ha.basis_note: Reduce deer/sheep for regeneration; wet ground is especially vulnerable to poaching.

sources.title: Woodland Trust grazing/regeneration guidance
sources.specific_claim: Browsing reduction is the primary regeneration action.

forage.nutritional_quality_note: Soil-builder; alder fixes nitrogen. No toxicity theme.

seasonal_forage_curve.winter: Low — keep off the soft surface

optimal_grazing_animals.notes: Keep stock off the soft wet surface; manage deer/sheep down.

unsuitable_animals.breed: Deer/sheep at hill densities; cattle on the wet surface
unsuitable_animals.reason: Prevent regeneration and poach the wet ground.

timing_windows.preferred_season: Reduce year-round browsing; keep off the soft surface
timing_windows.rationale: Allow regeneration; protect hydrology and soil structure

management.intensity_guidance: Reduce browsing; maintain wet character. Do not drain. Do not extend onto deep peat (rewet that instead).
management.regulatory_note: Do not drain. Bog woodland (w1d6) is shallow-peat only — NOT a route to afforest deep blanket bog (f1a), which would dry/oxidise the carbon store and breach peat policy. Keep deadwood; control invasives. Many wet woods are SSSI/SAC — check consents.
management.monitoring_protocol: Regeneration, water table/wet character, native dominance, poaching/drainage, deadwood. Apply the re-grade trigger.

bng_unit_value.value_note: Very high distinctiveness — priority habitat (H91E0/H91D0). High BNG value; ancient/alluvial stands irreplaceable.

sources.title: Statutory Biodiversity Metric
sources.specific_claim: Wet woodland very high distinctiveness; confirm exact label.

key_positive_indicators: wet-woodland ground flora and bryophytes; damp surface
integrity_indicators_fauna: rich damp-woodland invertebrate community (incl. deadwood/saproxylic species)
amphibians and wetland species on the wet floor
species_list.note: Skeleton only — full records in Phase 2B. Capture the f2b/h1b6 → w1d succession link and the deep-peat exclusion.

fields_fully_populated: ukhab_identity (w1d verified; w1d5/w1d6 Annex I types; deep-peat exclusion flagged)
fields_pending_research: Stand-level W4/W7/carr distinction and bog-woodland peat-depth checks
data_completeness.notes: The wet wooded endpoint of the rush-pasture/wet-heath succession. Bioregionally Wilder where new carr develops; ancient wet woodland Protect & Restore. CRITICAL: bog woodland (w1d6) is shallow-peat — never a justification to afforest deep blanket bog (f1a), where rewetting is the wilder move.

sources.title: UK Habitat Classification v2.01
sources.specific_claim: w1d wet woodland; w1d5 H91E0, w1d6 H91D0.

sources.title: Wilderculture field sheet 10 — Native Woodland
sources.specific_claim: Wet woodland ecology, zone, deep-peat exclusion.
